page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.96xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides dedicated CPU allocation with two threads per core.

Memory capacity totals 3072.0 GB, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. For local storage, the instance includes 22800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring massive memory footprints and high-speed local storage access. Suitable applications include large-scale in-memory databases, real-time analytics, big data processing, and enterprise-grade database management systems. The high density of memory and local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiencies by consolidating large workloads onto a single node, reducing inter-node network overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.96xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. Powered by Intel processors running at 3.9 GHz, it delivers 384 dedicated vCPUs across 192 physical cores with two threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, alongside 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This instance does not feature GPU acceleration. Designed for resource-intensive deployments, the r8idb.96xlarge is engineered for large-scale in-memory databases, extensive data warehousing, and high-throughput enterprise workloads that require substantial memory capacity and fast local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring three terabytes of RAM, massive local NVMe storage, and high-speed baseline networking for enterprise workloads.
